name: Mrs Fields Pecan Pie Bars
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dark brown sugar; firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a medium bowl, combine flour and chilled butter with a pastry cutter until the dough resembles coarse meal.
3. Gradually add water and mix just until the dough holds together and can be shaped into a ball. Alternatively, use a food processor fitted with a metal blade to combine the butter and flour until they resemble coarse meal. Add water in tablespoonfuls and process just until a dough ball begins to form.
4. Wrap the dough tightly in plastic wrap or place it in a plastic bag. Refrigerate for 1 hour or until firm.
5. On a floured board, using a floured rolling pin, roll out the dough into a 10x10-inch rectangle. Fold the dough in half and then into quarters. Place it in an 8x8-inch baking pan. Unfold the dough and press it into the corners and up along the sides of the pan. Refrigerate for 15 minutes.
6. To prepare the filling, melt 5 tablespoons of butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Remove from heat and stir in sugar and corn syrup until smooth. Add vanilla and eggs, and beat with a spoon until thoroughly combined. Fold in chopped pecans. Pour the pecan filling into the pastry-lined pan.
7. If the dough extends beyond the filling mixture, trim it with a knife. Place the pan in the center of the oven and bake for 50-60 minutes or until the filling is set. Cool on a wire rack.
8. Cut into 2x2-inch squares and top each with a pecan half. Serve at room temperature or chilled.
